extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIC attacked with 6km remaining on Col de Turini, absorbed breakaway remnants, then accelerated in final 200m to win and extend GC lead.
tactical_patterns: Adam Yates initiated GC fireworks at 7km triggering counterattacks, but initiators rarely survive summit finish wars of attrition.
Roglic attacked at 6km not final kilometer, suggesting he assessed rivals cracked earlier than traditional 1-2km launch zones.
INEOS split resources between Dennis pacing and Yates attacking, diluting firepower compared to Jumbo's singular Roglic focus.
Quintana repeatedly dropped then recovered shows tactical stalemate exploitation works when no team controls tempo in finale.

rider_signals.YATES Adam: negative: attacked 7km out to animate race but caught immediately, brother Simon far stronger on same climb
rider_signals.MARTINEZ Danny: positive: matched every Roglic acceleration and took second, marking work flawless despite no stage win threat
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: bridged to leaders after brother dropped, best GC result despite starting chase from behind
rider_signals.JORGENSEN Mateo: negative: lost white jersey by cracking early on climb, couldn't match GC pace despite previous time trial strength
rider_signals.ALMEIDA Joao: positive: recovered from poor week start to bridge late and take fifth, climbing form arriving mid-stage race
rider_signals.DENNIS Rowan: neutral: paced Roglic deep into climb showing domestique reliability, unusual role for former time trial world champion
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: neutral: repeatedly gapped but clawed back suggests tactical nous exceeds pure climbing power at this career stage
